intro: Pardoglossum cheirifolium is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the mountainous regions of China. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pardoglossum cheirifolium include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used to make capsules and tablets, as well as being incorporated into various herbal teas and tonics.
